title: Erigeron remyi
intro: Erigeron glabellus, also known as Rye Balm or Erigeron remyi, is a flowering plant species within the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Erigeron remyi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Erigeron remyi is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and salves, to promote wound healing and alleviate symptoms of respiratory tract infections.

benefits: Erigeron remyi is known for its potential health benefits, particularly in the Pacific region where it is native. properties: Erigeron remyi, also known as Hawaiian fleabane, is a plant that has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine. Its therapeutic properties are primarily related to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various skin conditions and wounds. The plant's leaves and stems can be made into a poultice or infused into a tea to apply topical relief. The plant's antiseptic properties may also help to prevent infection and promote healing in wounds.
